note: Official Intelligence Community strategy for open-source intelligence governance, integration, source discovery, data, tools, tradecraft, and workforce priorities.
title: The IC OSINT Strategy 2024-2026

note: The official "About Us" page of the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA), a U.S. Department of Defense and intelligence community organization. It describes NGA's mission of delivering geospatial intelligence, or GEOINT, to support military operations, policymakers, and first responders, spanning imagery analysis, mapping, geodesy, and navigation safety. The page also covers the agency's leadership, its establishment in 2003 from predecessor organizations, and its core values.
title: National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency About Us

note: An official NIST page on software supply chain security issued under Executive Order 14028, focused on the Software Bill of Materials as a formal record of software components and their supply chain. It explains the benefits of SBOMs for vulnerability identification and supply-chain transparency, recommends machine-readable formats such as SPDX, CycloneDX, and SWID, and describes foundational, sustaining, and enhancing levels of implementation. It cautions that SBOMs should complement rather than replace existing security practices.
title: Software Security in Supply Chains

note: NIST SP 800-218, the Secure Software Development Framework Version 1.1 published in February 2022, establishes a set of high-level practices for integrating security into software development lifecycles in order to reduce vulnerabilities in released software, mitigate the impact of exploited vulnerabilities, and address root causes to prevent recurrences. The framework is designed to serve both software developers implementing secure practices and software acquirers communicating security requirements to suppliers during procurement. It is intended to be flexible and technology-agnostic, applicable across programming languages, development methodologies, and operational environments. The publication complements existing secure development guidelines and supports compliance with executive-level directives on software supply chain security.
title: Secure Software Development Framework (SSDF) Version 1.1: Recommendations for Mitigating the Risk of Software Vulnerabilities

note: Official NIST NCCoE DevSecOps project page for software factory, secure pipeline, and continuous authorization source support.
title: DevSecOps

note: Official CISA Artificial Intelligence page for AI security, safety, resilience, and critical-infrastructure governance source support.
title: Artificial Intelligence

note: Official CISA Industrial Control Systems recommended practices page for defensive ICS/OT safety, resilience, and incident-preparation guidance.
title: ICS Recommended Practices

note: An archived CISA publication, "CISA Insights: Preparing for and Mitigating Foreign Influence Operations Targeting Critical Infrastructure," providing guidance on the threat that foreign influence campaigns pose to U.S. critical infrastructure. It explains how malicious actors use misinformation, disinformation, and malinformation (MDM) to shape opinion, undermine trust, and sow division, and how networked communications create new vectors that can amplify a narrative's impact over time. The document frames these operations as a risk to National Critical Functions, heightened by polarization and other social factors.
title: Preparing for and Mitigating Foreign Influence Operations Targeting Critical Infrastructure

note: An OASIS standard specification defining STIX (Structured Threat Information Expression), a language for exchanging cyber threat intelligence in a standardized, machine-readable form. It establishes a graph-based model with STIX Domain Objects, Cyber-observable Objects, and Relationship Objects, plus meta objects, bundles, and a patterning language for detection. JSON is the mandatory serialization format, and the specification is transport-agnostic and commonly paired with TAXII for distribution.
title: STIX Version 2.1

note: The OASIS Standard specification for TAXII (Trusted Automated Exchange of Intelligence Information) Version 2.1, published in 2021 by the OASIS Cyber Threat Intelligence Technical Committee. It defines a RESTful, HTTPS-based API protocol for sharing cyber threat intelligence between organizations, supporting two communication models: Collections (request-response) and Channels (publish-subscribe). The specification details JSON serialization, authentication, content negotiation, and API endpoints for discovery, collections metadata, object retrieval with filtering and pagination, and status monitoring, designed for scalable, interoperable threat-intelligence sharing and commonly paired with STIX-formatted content.
title: TAXII Version 2.1

note: An official NATO topic page describing the Alliance's approach to countering information threats, defined as intentional, manipulative, and coordinated activities by state and non-state actors including disinformation and propaganda. It explains why such threats matter for democratic processes and institutional trust, and outlines a four-part framework of understanding, preventing, containing, and recovering. The page emphasizes transparent communication, pre-bunking, coordination with allies and platforms, and protecting freedom of expression.
title: Countering Information Threats

note: Official Government of Canada guide for responsible use of agentic AI, used for public-sector agent governance and accountability source support.
title: Guide on the Use of Agentic Artificial Intelligence
